prs.title: fix: contribution Guide button on Dashboard redirects to incorrect page
prs.body: Description - Fixes the incorrect redirect for the Contribution Guide button on the Dashboard. - Updates the link to point to the correct AsyncAPI contribution guidelines page. - Improves navigation consistency and contributor onboarding experience. Fixes #4885 Now Redirecting to correct page ## Summary by CodeRabbit * **Chores** * Updated the "Contribution Guide" button to direct users to the AsyncAPI site contribution guidelines page. ✏️ Tip: You can customize this high-level summary in your review settings.

prs.title: Fix inconsistent spacing between channel privacy icon and name
prs.body:
This PR fixes inconsistent spacing between the channel privacy icon and the channel name by removing legacy HTML whitespace
from the inline_decorated_channel_name.hbs partial.

Spacing is now handled consistently via an em-based margin on
.channel-privacy-type-icon, avoiding unpredictable gaps across
different render locations.

Fixes: https://github.com/zulip/zulip/issues/36987

This change was reviewed by inspecting the shared
inline_decorated_channel_name.hbs partial and verifying that spacing
is now handled solely via CSS on .channel-privacy-type-icon.

The change is intentionally limited to shared styling logic to ensure
consistent behavior across modal headers, dropdowns, sidebar stream
lists, and settings views without introducing regressions.


This change affects spacing consistency across shared UI components
and does not introduce new UI elements. Screenshots will be added if
requested during review.

prs.title: Update Readme.md with overview section
prs.body:
What I Did

Updated the README.md to improve clarity, structure, and onboarding experience

Added a new Overview section that explains what the website does and what users can expect

Enhanced readability with clear headings for Games and Activities

Made the documentation smoother and more helpful for beginners and contributors

Improvements Made

Added Overview section with detailed explanation of Games & Activities

Improved formatting and section flow

Ensured the content is beginner-friendly and aligned with project goals

prs.title: Image and text issue of general secretary and chairman i resolved for…

labels.name: size:S
labels.description: This PR changes 10-29 lines, ignoring generated files

prs.body:
## **User description** What I Did Unhid the Leadership section on mobile devices Implemented responsive layout improvements to ensure proper visibility and structure on smaller screens Added image scaling and flexible text sizes for better readability on mobile Prevented layout overlap issues by conditionally hiding decorative background elements on small screens Changes Made Enabled visibility of Leadership section for mobile breakpoints Adjusted CSS for responsive spacing, font sizing, and image proportions Applied conditional styling to avoid visual clutter and overlap Result Leadership section is now fully visible and readable on mobile Clean and scalable UI without overlapping elements Enhanced mobile experience while maintaining desktop design integrity ___ ## **CodeAnt-AI Description** **Display Leadership section on mobile and fix chairman/general secretary image and text layout** ### What Changed - Leadership section is now shown on mobile devices (previously hidden on small screens). - Decorative background shapes are hidden on small screens to prevent them from overlapping portraits and text. - Leader photos now scale to the screen with sensible max widths, rounded corners, and shadows so they no longer overflow or crop on phones. - Text sizes for names, titles, and messages use responsive, fixed-size steps so content is readable across screen sizes instead of relying on viewport units. - General Secretary section heading corrected to "Message from the General Secretary". - On small screens the General Secretary image and text order was adjusted so the image appears above the text for a clearer reading flow. ### Impact `✅ Leadership visible on phones` `✅ No overlapping images and backgrounds on small screens` `✅ Readable leader photos and names on mobile`
💡 Usage Guide ### Checking Your Pull Request Every time you make a pull request, our system automatically looks through it. We check for security issues, mistakes in how you're setting up your infrastructure, and common code problems. We do this to make sure your changes are solid and won't cause any trouble later. ### Talking to CodeAnt AI Got a question or need a hand with something in your pull request? You can easily get in touch with CodeAnt AI right here. Just type the following in a comment on your pull request, and replace "Your question here" with whatever you want to ask: 
@codeant-ai ask: Your question here
This lets you have a chat with CodeAnt AI about your pull request, making it easier to understand and improve your code. #### Example 
@codeant-ai ask: Can you suggest a safer alternative to storing this secret?
### Preserve Org Learnings with CodeAnt You can record team preferences so CodeAnt AI applies them in future reviews. Reply directly to the specific CodeAnt AI suggestion (in the same thread) and replace "Your feedback here" with your input: 
@codeant-ai: Your feedback here
This helps CodeAnt AI learn and adapt to your team's coding style and standards. #### Example 
@codeant-ai: Do not flag unused imports.
### Retrigger review Ask CodeAnt AI to review the PR again, by typing: 
@codeant-ai: review
### Check Your Repository Health To analyze the health of your code repository, visit our dashboard at [https://app.codeant.ai](https://app.codeant.ai). This tool helps you identify potential issues and areas for improvement in your codebase, ensuring your repository maintains high standards of code health.

prs.title: Sorting game #23
prs.body:
🧩 What I Added

Language: Python 

Feature: Sorting Visualization Race Game using Tkinter

Description:
Added a visual sorting race game that compares Bubble Sort, Merge Sort, and Quick Sort in real time using Tkinter.
It also includes a fun betting mode, where the user guesses which algorithm will finish sorting first.

Features

✅ Visualizes Bubble, Merge, and Quick Sort algorithms
✅ Uses threading so all run “simultaneously”
✅ Includes a betting mode for interactivity
✅ Color-coded bars for different states:

🔵 Normal

🔴 Comparing/swapping

🟣 Merging

🟩 Sorted

How to Run in terminal:
Go to Sorting_game folder and run file like (python sorting_game.py)

Notes for Reviewers

Simple and beginner-friendly visualization

Designed to help understand sorting algorithms intuitively

Requires only the built-in Tkinter module

prs.title: Todo-list in Javascript
prs.body:
What I added

Language: JavaScript

Short description: Added a simple To-Do List project inside the JavaScript folder. It includes basic functionalities like adding new tasks, deleting tasks, and marking tasks as completed. The interface is styled with a dark bluish theme for a clean and modern look.

How to run:

Open the todo-list folder.

Run index.html directly in your browser or use VS Code’s Live Server extension.

Notes for reviewers

The project is kept simple and beginner-friendly.

Uses plain JavaScript, CSS, and HTML no external dependencies.

Easily extendable for features like task persistence or filters.

prs.title: This PR adjusts top and bottom padding across multiple pages and components to ensure consistent spacing below the fixed Navbar and improve layout hierarchy. It also fixes minor semantic and Tailwind class issues.
prs.body:
1. Hero Component (src/components/Hero.jsx)

Changed padding from py-14 (56px top/bottom) → pt-28 pb-14 (112px top, 56px bottom)

Adds proper spacing to accommodate the fixed navbar

2. Programs Page (src/pages/Programs.jsx)

Fixed invalid Tailwind class py-15 → pt-32 pb-16 (128px top, 64px bottom)

Updated sticky filter offset from top-0 → top-20 (80px) to position correctly below the navbar

3. Learning Track Page (src/pages/LearningTrack.jsx)

Updated padding from py-16 → pt-28 pb-16 (112px top, 64px bottom)

4. Community Page (src/pages/CommunityPage.jsx)

Replaced nested <main> with <div> to fix semantic issues

Updated padding from py-12 → pt-28 pb-12 (112px top, 48px bottom)

Hero padding updated to pt-12 pb-6

5. Mentors Page (src/pages/Mentors.jsx)

Updated padding from py-16 → pt-32 pb-16 (128px top, 64px bottom)

6. Contributors Page (src/pages/Contributors.jsx)

Updated padding from py-12 → pt-28 pb-12 (112px top, 48px bottom)

Loading state updated to pt-28

7. Module Detail Page (src/pages/ModuleDetail.jsx)

Updated padding from pt-20 → pt-28 for both normal and error states

<!-- This is an auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->
## Summary by CodeRabbit

* **Style**
  * Refined vertical spacing and padding across multiple pages and header sections for improved visual consistency and layout balance.
  * Increased top spacing on several headers and loading states to reduce crowding and improve hierarchy.
  * Adjusted sticky filter bar vertical position for a smoother, more predictable scrolling experience.
  * Updated header text colors and reduced dark-mode gradient variants for a more consistent theme; loading state message now displays "Loading contributors from GitHub..."
<!-- end of auto-generated comment: release notes by coderabbit.ai -->

issues.title: [DESIGN]: Improve “On this page” navigation to include Docs category sections
issues.body: ### Describe the Issue On the Docs landing page, the “On this page” section currently lists only the top-level headings and does not include navigation links for the main category sections displayed on the page. The page visually presents the following major sections: - Concepts - Tutorials - Guides - Tools - Reference - Migration - Community - Specification Explorer However, these sections are not reflected as anchor links in the “On this page” navigation. ### Expected Outcome The “On this page” section should include anchor links for each major category section visible on the page, allowing users to quickly jump to them. ### Screenshots ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the AsyncAPI documentation homepage 2. Observe the “On this page” section on the right-hand side 3. Scroll through the main content area 4. Notice that category sections (Concepts, Tutorials, Guides, etc.) are not listed as navigation links in the “On this page” section ### Browser/Device OS: macOS Browser: Chrome ### Additional Notes _No response_ ### Check Before Submitting - [x] I have looked for similar issues to avoid duplicates. - [x] I have reviewed the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md). ### Would you like to help fix this issue? (Not required, but appreciated!) - [x] Yes, I am interested in contributing to the fix. - [x] No, I am just reporting the issue.

issues.title: [DESIGN]: Improve readability of and layout structure of Roadmap page by grouping goals columns into cards
issues.body: ### Describe the Issue The current **Goals** section in Roadmap page on the AsyncAPI website displays three columns of text without any visual container or grouping. While the content itself is valuable, the lack of visual separation makes the section harder to scan, especially on larger screens, and slightly overwhelming for first-time users. I’d like to propose a small UI/UX enhancement to improve readability, structure, and responsiveness. ### Expected Outcome Wrap each goal inside a card-style container, similar to the layout shown in the second screenshot. 1. Improves visual hierarchy and readability 2. Makes each goal feel like a distinct, scannable unit 3. Enhances UX consistency with other card-based sections on the site 4. Helps users quickly understand and compare goals ### Screenshots Currently it look like this: After fixing it: small screen size: ### Steps to Reproduce 1. Open the AsyncAPI website 2. Navigate to the Roadmap page where the three **Goals** are displayed 3. Observe the current text-only column layout 4. Compare with the proposed card-based layout in the attached screenshot ### Browser/Device OS: macOS Browser: Chrome Browser Version: Latest ### Additional Notes _No response_ ### Check Before Submitting - [x] I have looked for similar issues to avoid duplicates. - [x] I have reviewed the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md). ### Would you like to help fix this issue? (Not required, but appreciated!) - [x] Yes, I am interested in contributing to the fix. - [x] No, I am just reporting the issue.

issues.title: [BUG] “Contributing” link in Community navbar dropdown redirects to incorrect page
issues.body: ### Describe the bug. In the top navigation bar, when hovering over Community, the “Contributing” link redirects to an incorrect page instead of the intended AsyncAPI contribution guidelines. This affects discoverability for new contributors who use the navigation menu to find contribution-related information. ### Expected behavior Clicking Community → Contributing from the navbar dropdown should redirect users to the official contribution guidelines page: https://www.asyncapi.com/docs/community/010-contribution-guidelines ### Screenshots Redirects to- should be redirect to- ### How to Reproduce 1. Visit https://www.asyncapi.com/ 2. Hover over Community in the top navigation bar 3. Click on Contributing 4. Observe that the link redirects to the wrong page ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Operating System (OS): macOs - Browser: Chrome - Browser Version: ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? Yes I am willing to submit a PR!

issues.title: [BUG] Contribution Guide button on Community Dashboard redirects to incorrect page

labels.name: triaged
labels.description: Issue has been reviewed and discussed

issues.body: ### Describe the bug. On the Community → Dashboard page, the “Contribution Guide” button redirects to an incorrect page instead of the expected AsyncAPI contribution guidelines. Other contribution-related links (e.g. “View on GitHub”) work correctly, but this specific button does not point to the intended documentation, which may confuse new contributors trying to get started. From a UX perspective, this breaks consistency and the user’s mental model of navigation. ### Expected behavior Clicking the Contribution Guide button should redirect users to the official AsyncAPI contribution guidelines, for example: - asyncapi/community/CONTRIBUTING.md or the equivalent rendered documentation page ### Screenshots Community Dashboard showing the Contribution Guide button Page where the button currently redirects Expected destination (CONTRIBUTING.md) which other links already use ### How to Reproduce 1. Go to AsyncAPI website 2. Navigate to Community → Dashboard 3. Click on the Contribution Guide button 4. Observe that the page opened is not the expected contribution guidelines ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Operating System (OS):macOS - Browser:Chrome:Chrome - Browser Version: ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? Yes I am willing to submit a PR!

issues.title: [BUG] Broken link to AsyncAPI contributing guidelines on -docs/community/onboarding page
issues.body: ### Describe the bug. On the "Technical writer onboarding guide" page, the link to the AsyncAPI contributing guidelines appears to be broken and currently returns a Page does not exist. This can be confusing for new contributors, as the page explicitly directs them to the contributing guidelines for further information. ### Expected behavior Clicking the "AsyncAPI contributing guidelines" link should navigate to a valid page with the contribution guidelines. ### Screenshots Redirects to- ### How to Reproduce 1. Open the Technical writer onboarding guide page. 2. Click on the "AsyncAPI contributing guidelines" link. 3. Observe that it leads to a 404 Not Found page. ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Operating System (OS): macOS - Browser: Chrome - Browser Version: Latest ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? issues.title: [BUG] Inconsistent footer alignment on small screen sizes
issues.body: ### Describe the bug. On smaller viewports (mobile screen sizes), the footer layout appears visually misaligned, with content leaning toward the left and spacing that feels inconsistent compared to larger screen sizes. From a UI perspective, this creates an unbalanced visual hierarchy and makes the footer harder to scan and interpret on mobile devices, where alignment and spacing play a critical role in readability and usability. ### Expected behavior The footer layout should maintain clear visual alignment and consistent spacing across viewport sizes, ensuring a balanced and intentional UI presentation on mobile screens. This would improve design consistency, visual rhythm, and overall user experience, especially for users navigating the site on smaller devices. ### Screenshots ### How to Reproduce 1. Open the AsyncAPI website in a browser. 2. Resize the browser window to a mobile viewport width (less than 768px) 3. Scroll down to the footer section. 4. Observe the alignment and spacing of the footer content on the mobile view. ### 🖥️ Device Information [optional] - Operating System (OS): macOS - Browser: Google Chrome - Browser Version: Latest ### 👀 Have you checked for similar open issues? - [x] I checked and didn't find similar issue ### 🏢 Have you read the Contributing Guidelines? - [x] I have read the [Contributing Guidelines](https://github.com/asyncapi/.github/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.md) ### Are you willing to work on this issue ? Yes I am willing to submit a PR!
